2606.09236.title: Self-Paced Curriculum Reinforcement Learning for Autonomous Superbike Racing in Simulation
2606.09236.paper_summary: Autonomous Racing has seen remarkable progress through deep Reinforcement Learning (RL), primarily for four-wheeled vehicles. However, motorbikes introduce substantially greater complexity due to the need to manage balance and lean angle, in addition to more reactive steering and throttle control, and a smaller weight. In this work, we present a framework for training an autonomous agent to race a superbike in VRider SBK, a physics-accurate Unity-based motorbike simulator. Our approach integrates Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) with Self-Paced curriculum Deep reinforcement Learning (SPDL), which dynamically generates progressively more challenging tasks based on the agent's performance, without requiring manual curriculum design. The agent's state space comprises proprioceptive features extended with lean-angle history, along with global track features via course points. The reward signal is shaped to encourage progress along the track while penalizing instability-inducing behaviors specific to two-wheeled dynamics. Preliminary experimental results demonstrate that SPDL outperforms SAC alone in training efficiency, lap time, and driving stability across multiple tracks and motorbike models, establishing a first baseline for RL-based autonomous motorbike racing.
2606.09236.paper_summary_zh: 自主賽車在深度強化學習（RL）的推動下取得了顯著進展，主要針對四輪車輛。然而，摩托車因為需要管理平衡和傾斜角度，除了更具反應性的轉向和油門控制外，還有更小的重量，帶來了更大的複雜性。在這項工作中，我們提出了一個框架，用於訓練自主代理在VRider SBK中駕駛超級摩托車，這是一個基於Unity的物理準確摩托車模擬器。我們的方法將Soft Actor-Critic（SAC）與自我調整課程深度強化學習（SPDL）相結合，該方法根據代理的表現動態生成逐步更具挑戰性的任務，而無需手動設計課程。代理的狀態空間包括擴展了傾斜角度歷史的本體感知特徵，以及通過課程點獲得的全局賽道特徵。獎勵信號被設計為鼓勵沿著賽道的進展，同時懲罰特定於兩輪動力學的導致不穩定的行為。初步實驗結果顯示，SPDL在訓練效率、圈速和駕駛穩定性方面超越了單獨的SAC，並在多條賽道和摩托車模型中建立了基於RL的自主摩托車賽車的第一個基準。
2606.09236.author: Luca Ghisi et.al.
2606.09236.authors: Luca Ghisi, Jacopo Essenziale, Carlo D'Eramo, Matteo Luperto
